title: Middle Eastern Chicken Musakhan
description: A herbal and creamy Middle Eastern dish featuring sweetened condensed milk, this crowd-pleaser is high in protein and perfect for special occasions. It is a traditional musakhan recipe with a twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together sumac, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken breasts to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the sumac mixture.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the chicken breasts until browned on both sides.
Transfer the chicken to a baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for 25 minutes.
While the chicken is baking, heat the chopped onions in a skillet over medium heat until they are softened and caramelized.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the sweetened condensed milk and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce for 5 minutes.
To assemble the musakhan, slice the cooked chicken breasts and place them on top of the pita bread pieces.
Spoon the onion and sweetened condensed milk sauce over the chicken and garnish with chopped parsley.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
